column1: # persisted-paths.tsv - single source of truth for personal-features' persisted
# bind-mount paths. Adding, removing, or renaming a persisted path means editing
# THIS file; every consumer below derives from it, and CI fails if any of them
# drifts out of sync.
# Consumers:
#   install.sh               creates each container_target (mkdir -p / chown / chmod mode)
#   setup.sh                 creates each host_source under the host home directory
#   setup.ps1                mirrors setup.sh row-for-row (hand-kept in sync; no pwsh CI
#                            for the container build, so parity is checked statically by
#                            .github/scripts/test_host_setup_parity.py)
#   check_persisted_paths.py asserts devcontainer-feature.json's mounts + containerEnv
#                            match these rows. The JSON cannot be generated from this
#                            file: release-please patches $.version into the checked-in
#                            JSON in place, so it must stay hand-authored and be checked.
# Tab-separated. Columns:
#   name             stable identifier for the path (docs / error messages only)
#   host_source      bind-mount source, relative to the host home dir ($HOME / %USERPROFILE%)
#   container_target bind-mount target (absolute path inside the container)
#   env_var          containerEnv variable that points at the target, or - if none
#   env_value        exact value of env_var in the JSON, or - when env_var is -
#   mode             octal permissions install.sh applies to container_target
# A trailing slash marks a DIRECTORY (created with mkdir -p); no trailing slash
# marks a FILE (created with touch, after mkdir -p of its parent). Docker
# materialises a *missing* single-file mount source as a directory, which then
# fails the mount, so file sources must be touched into existence - hence the
# distinction. All six paths are directories today.
# name
claude
gh
odoo_sdk
pr-automation
coderabbit
shell-history
